title: Hemionitis hydornii
intro: Hemionitis hydronii, commonly known as the water fern or golden spleenwort, is a species of fern native to tropical regions of Asia and Australia. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various health issues such as fever, skin infections, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Hemionitis hydronii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make various herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, as well as to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Hemionitis hydornii is classified within the family Polypodiaceae. It belongs to the genus Hemionitis. This species is further categorized as a type of fern. Hemionitis hydornii is native to tropical regions of Asia and Australia. Within the plant kingdom, it is classified as a vascular plant with true leaves.
uses: Hemionitis hydornii, also known as the okepe fern or kidney fern, is a medicinal plant traditionally used in various cultures. Its extracts have been used to treat various health issues, including kidney problems, digestive issues, and fever. In some parts of the world, it is also believed to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential side effects. side_effects: Hemionitis hydorii is not a widely known plant name however I found some information regarding Hemionitis hydorii's relation to Hemionitis lyrata  which is a plant with a few known side effects.
